info.title: Workyard Developer API
info.description:
# Overview

The Workyard REST API (https://api.workyard.com) allows you to directly perform operations on the following objects:

* Employees (W2 and 1099)
* Customers
* Projects
* Cost Codes
* Geofences
* Tasks
* File Attachments
* Tags
* Time Cards

## Authentication

In order to access the Workyard API you will need to include an API token in the `Authorization` header of your requests.

Follow the steps below to obtain and authenticate with an API token:

1. **Obtain API Token:**
    - If you are an admin, go to **Integrations —> API Token** from your Workyard dashboard
    - Click on **Generate API Token**
    - Select the expiry period for the token. It is set to 60 months by default.
    - Copy the API Token.

1. **Include API Token in Requests:**
    - Include the API token in the `Authorization` header of your API requests using the `Bearer` scheme.
    - Replace `YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN` with the actual token obtained.

## Rate Limits

To ensure fair usage and maintain API stability, the Workyard API implements a limit of 60 requests per minute.

If you exceed the limit the API will return a HTTP 429 response.

The response will include a `Retry-After` header with a value of `1` second.

## Concurrency Limits

Some endpoints are limited to **one in-flight request at a time per API user, per endpoint**.

The following endpoints currently enforce this limit:

* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/projects/{org_project_id}/cost_codes`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/employees`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/employees.v2`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/time_off_requests`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/tasks`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/tasks/{task_id}/activity`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/tasks/{task_id}/checklists`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/projects/{org_project_id}/tasks`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/time_cards`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/time_cards_activity_segments`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/active_geofences`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/geofences`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/smart_forms/{form_id}/submissions`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/customers`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/projects`
* `GET /orgs/{org_id}/geofences/{geofence_id}/projects`

If a second request to the same endpoint is issued while a prior request from your API user is still being processed, the API will return a 429 response.
The response will include a `Retry-After` header with a value of `30` seconds. Sequential calls to the same endpoint are unaffected — once the in-flight request completes, subsequent calls succeed immediately.

This limit is per-endpoint, so you can run one `time_cards` request alongside one `time_cards_activity_segments` request without collision.

## Best Practices

Following these practices will keep your integration reliable and prevent your API token from being throttled. A correct, well-behaved integration that finishes a few seconds slower is a better outcome than a fast one that trips the rate limit and fails partway through.

* **Make sequential requests, not parallel ones.** Issue one request at a time and wait for each response before sending the next. Do not use thread pools, worker pools, `asyncio.gather`, `Promise.all`, goroutines, or any other concurrency mechanism to send Workyard API calls simultaneously. This is mandatory for the concurrency-limited endpoints listed above, and recommended everywhere.

* **Paginate sequentially.** To page through a large collection (projects, time cards, employees, etc.), fetch page N, finish processing the response, then fetch page N+1. Do not pre-fetch or request multiple pages at once. Because many list endpoints allow only one in-flight request at a time per API user (see [Concurrency Limits](#concurrency-limits)), parallel pagination will fail with HTTP 429.

* **Stay comfortably under the limit.** The ceiling is 60 requests per minute. Aim for that — roughly one request per second or slower — and add a small delay between requests rather than bursting up to the limit.

* **Implement exponential backoff** when you receive a 429 response, and respect the `Retry-After` header when present. Treat a 429 as a signal to slow down for the rest of the run, not just to retry the one failed request.

* **Reduce the number of calls you make.** Use the smallest page size that meets your need, apply available filters and date ranges to narrow the result set, and cache responses to avoid re-fetching data that has not changed.

* **Monitor your API usage** to ensure you stay within the rate limits.

### A note on AI-generated integration code

If you use an AI assistant or code-generation tool to build your integration, be aware that these tools often default to high-throughput, multi-threaded designs — for example, fetching every page of a large result set in parallel. Against this API that pattern is counter-productive: it trips the rate and concurrency limits and causes requests to fail.

When prompting an AI tool to write Workyard API code, explicitly instruct it to:

* issue requests sequentially, one at a time, with no parallelism, threading, or async fan-out;
* paginate one page after another, waiting for each response before requesting the next;
* keep the request rate below 60 per minute, with a deliberate delay between calls;
* implement exponential backoff that honors the `Retry-After` header on any 429 response.

Optimize the generated code for correctness and good API citizenship, not for speed.

get.description: Get employees in an organization with filters applied

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status. Standard filter format, only valid operator is `(eq)` and valid values are (`active`,`removed`,`saas_disabled`), `','` is valid for passing multiple values (e.g. `eq:active` - `eq:active,removed`)

parameters.name: employee_id
parameters.description: Filter by employee_id which uniquely identifies an employee record. Standard filter format, only valid operator is `(eq)`,`','` is valid for passing multiple values (e.g. `eq:12345` - `eq:12345,45678`)

parameters.name: employee_group_id
parameters.description: Filter by employee group IDs (team IDs). Standard filter format, only valid operator is `(eq)`,`','` is valid for passing multiple values (e.g. `eq:5` - `eq:5,7,9`). When both employee_id and employee_group_id are provided, uses OR logic.

parameters.name: profile_id
parameters.description: Filter by profile_id, which uniquely identifies an individual. Standard filter format, only valid operator is `(eq)`,`','` is valid for passing multiple values (e.g. `eq:12345` - `eq:12345,45678`)

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Filter by email. Standard filter format, only valid operator is `(eq)`,`','` is valid for passing multiple values (e.g. `eq:john@test.com` - `eq:john@test.com,katie@test.com`)

parameters.name: employee_display_name
parameters.description: Filter by name (first and last name). Standard filter format, only valid operators are `(eq|like)` and valid values are alphabetic strings or alphabetic strings with percent signs, `','` is valid for passing multiple values (e.g. `eq:Ma hdi` - `eq:jason` - `like:jason%`), exact case insensitive match

parameters.name: has_time_card_start_dt_unix
parameters.description: Filter for employees having time cards within the given start_dt range. Standard filter format, only valid operators is `(gte|lt)` and valid values are unix timestamps

parameters.name: has_assigned_tasks_display_dt_unix
parameters.description:
Filter for employees having tasks within the given display_dt parameters, To specify a particular day, provide the timestamp.
 For a date range, provide a 2nd unix timestamp concatenated with a `+` (%2b).
 The results list date ranges and sorts are automatically determined by the backend. You cannot combine this
 parameter with search, other date filters or the sort filter.

parameters.name: has_pending_profile
parameters.description: Filter for employees with pending profiles. Standard filter format, only valid operator is `(eq)` and valid values are `(true|false)`. Example: `eq:true` or `eq:false`

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of relationships to include in the response. Valid values: `employee_groups`

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination batch size, default value is 100, use 0 for no limit (everything on one page)

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Sort order. Standard format, valid operators are `(asc|desc)` and valid values are `(employee_display_name|created_at|updated_at|activated_at|id)`, `'+'` is valid for passing multiple values. e.g. `desc:id`, `desc:employee_display_name+id`

rrule_string.description:
The recurring rule string, documented here: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5545
 Example:

 DTSTART;TZID=America/Los_Angeles:20230101T000000
 RRULE:FREQ=MONTHLY

 Note: The DTSTART is required, and the TZID must be a valid timezone. Until is always in UTC, as per the standards
